criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose anterior ischemia, which of the following diagnostic criterion should be evaluated?
options: Presence of ST-segment depression in leads II, III, and aVF
Presence of ST-segment depression in anterior leads
Presence of flattened T waves in inferior leads
Presence of diffuse concave ST-segment elevation in anterior leads
Presence of J-point elevation in anterior leads
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of ST-segment depression in anterior leads

finding.question: Reagrding the criterion you selected, does this ECG show ST depression in at least two of the anterior leads, including leads V2, V3, V4, and V5?

grounding.question: Which of the following leads show ST depression? Select all possible leads from the options below.
Within the selected leads, in which of the following segments can you observe the QRS complexes with ST depression? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: based on the finding identified above, does this patient have anterior ischemia?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
